title: Dependency Graphs, Incremental Builds, Caches, and Watch Mode · Contract Workshop
summary: A civic-publishing pair incrementally builds clean-equivalent incremental dependency engine from original fixed content fixtures while retaining earlier Python, Git, HTML, CSS, URL, accessibility, security, determinism, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of clean-equivalent incremental dependency engine while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Record source, metadata, template, include, collection, taxonomy, route, asset, configuration, and tool-version dependencies per output.
Key cached stages by canonical input bytes, relevant configuration, algorithm and tool versions, schema, and platform boundary.
Propagate changed, added, deleted, renamed, and dependency updates to every affected output and derived collection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dependency Graph
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop publishing team handles case 5919 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove dependency-aware incremental output is equivalent to a clean build; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dependency Graph.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for record source, metadata, template, include, collection, taxonomy, route, asset, configuration, and tool-version dependencies per output. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dependency Graph, keep Dependency Graph connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-clean-equivalence-mc9kdb
# Competency: ssg-clean-equivalence.
# Case 8679: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_clean_equivalence_mc9kdb(outputs: int = 20, dependency_nodes: int = 37, invalidated: int = 6, stale_outputs: int = 0, clean_digest_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_mc9kdb = "mc9kdb-bdk9cm-8679"
    assert evidence_variant_mc9kdb
    evidence_axes_mc9kdb = (
        "readermc9kdb",
        "source8679",
        "parsebdk9cm",
        "routemc9kdb8679",
        "artifact8679mc9kdb",
        "recoverybdk9cm8679",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_mc9kdb)) == 6
    if outputs < 1 or dependency_nodes < outputs:
        return False, dependency_nodes, "dependency-shape"
    if invalidated < 1 or invalidated > outputs:
        return False, invalidated, "invalidation-scope"
    if stale_outputs > 0:
        return False, stale_outputs, "stale-output"
    if not clean_digest_equal:
        return False, invalidated, "clean-drift"
    assert dependency_nodes >= outputs
    return True, invalidated, "clean-equivalent-incremental"
